name: Baked Ratatouille & Cheese Custard
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Teaspoon dried hot red pepper flakes
1 red bell pepper, halved into 1/2-inch pieces
1 yellow bell pepper, halved into 1/2-inch pieces
1 green bell pepper, halved into 1/2-inch pieces
1 Cup Cooked Chick-Peas (or Canned), Rinse and Drain
2/3 Cup fresh basil leaves, packed and chopped
2/3 Cup fresh parsley leaves, packed and chopped
Freshly Ground Black Pepper to Taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Make ratatouille: In a 12-inch non-stick skillet, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil over moderate heat until hot but not smoking. Cook half of the cubed eggplant, stirring frequently, until golden brown and tender (about 6 minutes). Transfer cooked eggplant to a gratin dish or shallow baking dish. Cook remaining eggplant in another 2 tablespoons oil in the same skillet and add it to the dish. In the skillet, cook onions, garlic, and red pepper flakes with the remaining tablespoon of oil over moderately low heat, stirring, until onions are softened. Add bell peppers and cook over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until tender (about 8 minutes). Stir in all other ratatouille ingredients and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes. Season the mixture with salt and additional pepper, then spoon it evenly over the eggplant. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Make cheese custard: In a heavy saucepan, melt butter over moderately low heat. Whisk in flour and cook the roux, whisking, for 3 minutes. Whisk in milk in a stream and salt and pepper to taste. Bring the sauce to a boil, whisking constantly, and simmer, whisking, for 2 minutes. Stir in Parmesan and remove the pan from heat. In a heatproof bowl, whisk egg yolks together and whisk in about one-fourth of the sauce. Whisk this yolk mixture into the remaining sauce.
3. Spoon the custard over the ratatouille. The ratatouille can be prepared up to this point up to 4 hours ahead and chilled, covered. Bake the ratatouille in the middle of the oven for 45 minutes, or until the custard is lightly browned. Cool the ratatouille for 10 minutes before serving. Serves 4 to 6 as a main course or 8 as a side dish.
